angles.title: Hybrid MIL-Autoencoder Framework for Anomaly Detection in Whale Vocalizations
angles.description: Develop a hybrid model combining Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) with Neural Network Autoencoder (NN-AE) to improve anomaly detection in whale vocalizations, addressing the challenge of false normal classifications and temporal localization.
angles.reasoning: This approach leverages the strengths of MIL for weakly supervised learning and NN-AE for anomaly detection, addressing limitations in current models related to false normal rates and temporal localization accuracy.

angles.title: Real-Time Whale Call Sequencing Using Contrastive Learning and Energy-Based Segmentation
angles.description: Implement a system that uses contrastive learning for robust feature extraction and energy-based segmentation to capture entire whale call sequences, addressing the issue of fragmented temporal frequency representations (TFRs).
angles.reasoning: Contrastive learning can enhance feature extraction by focusing on similarities and differences, while energy-based segmentation ensures accurate capture of entire call sequences, improving monitoring accuracy.
brief_plan: Develop a contrastive learning model for feature extraction
Enhance model for various network conditions
related_limitations: Challenges in capturing entire call sequences

angles.title: Adaptive Network for Multi-Species Acoustic Detection and Tracking
angles.description: Design an adaptive neural network model capable of dynamically adjusting to diverse marine soundscapes for multi-species detection and tracking, incorporating additional AI-based models for species diversity.
angles.reasoning: This approach addresses the need for models that can handle diverse species and soundscapes, potentially revolutionizing monitoring efforts by providing a flexible solution adaptable to various conditions.
brief_plan: Develop an adaptive neural network architecture
related_limitations: Need for AI integration for species diversity
